title: Syncarpha marlothii
intro: Syncarpha marlothii, also known as the 'Tall-club', is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family Asteraceae. This plant has been traditionally used in Southern African folk medicine, boasting a range of health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in reducing inflammation and fighting off infections. The bioactive constituents of Syncarpha marlothii include sesquiterpenoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used to make herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often prepared to alleviate symptoms of fever, colds, and respiratory issues.
taxonomy: Syncarpha marlothii is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is a member of the tribe Gnaphalieae and is native to South Africa. The plant is known for its distinctive appearance and is often found in dry areas. Syncarpha marlothii is a shrubby perennial herb that produces small white flowers. Its classification also includes being in the genus Syncarpha, which is a group of plants that are commonly found in the southern regions of Africa.
